title: Four Against Darkness - Deep Dive Part 1 - Introduction
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=900BOlBTFNg

Daniel (@dungeondive) kicks off an ambitious deep dive into Four Against Darkness, the delightful solo dungeon-crawling roll-and-write game. Rather than jumping straight into the base game review, this inaugural episode maps out the entire journey ahead: every official supplement, fan creation, and expansion the channel plans to explore in exhaustive detail.

The video showcases Daniel's growing collection of FAD materials, from established releases like Fiendish Foes and Caves of the Kobold Slave Masters to anticipated titles he's ordered but hasn't yet received. What's particularly charming is his willingness to engage the community—he explicitly asks viewers to recommend their favorite supplements and help prioritize which lesser-known expansions deserve coverage. You also get a glimpse of his game in progress, complete with myth miniatures representing his party of Santé (monk), Mork (barbarian), Mara (cleric), and Jarrow (rogue), all marching through dungeons with handmade character sheets and cheat sheets.

Beyond the logistical roadmap, Daniel articulates why Four Against Darkness has become his personal antidote to stress. The game's meditative rhythm—rolling, looking up tables, mapping, and reacting—creates an almost zen-like flow state that's perfect for someone juggling a hectic life. He promises the series will take several months to unfold, with one or two books examined per episode depending on complexity.

----

What's your go-to solo game when life gets overwhelming? Have you tackled any FAD supplements you think deserve special attention in this deep dive?
